data_points.claim: Davis 2020 breakthrough-DMT entity survey sample size
data_points.detail: Anonymous online survey of people who took a 'breakthrough' dose of inhaled N,N-DMT and encountered a seemingly autonomous being/entity; Johns Hopkins (Davis, Clifton, Weaver, Hurwitz, Johnson, Griffiths), J Psychopharmacol 2020. Recruited from >10,000 initial respondents.

data_points.claim: Most common label used for the DMT entity was 'being'
data_points.detail: Entity-descriptor breakdown, Davis 2020 (N=2561). 'Being' was the single most frequent label.

data_points.claim: Other frequent DMT entity labels: guide / spirit / alien / helper
data_points.detail: Entity-descriptor breakdown, Davis 2020. Respondents could endorse multiple labels; more specific labels like 'elf' were far rarer than generic 'being/guide/helper'.

data_points.claim: DMT entity perceived as conscious
data_points.detail: Attribute ratings of the encountered entity, Davis 2020 (N=2561).

data_points.claim: DMT entity perceived as intelligent
data_points.detail: Attribute ratings, Davis 2020.

data_points.claim: DMT entity perceived as benevolent
data_points.detail: Attribute ratings, Davis 2020. Contrasts with only ~41% reporting any fear during the encounter.

data_points.claim: DMT entity perceived as sacred
data_points.detail: Attribute ratings, Davis 2020. A separate 54% rated the entity as having agency in the world.

data_points.claim: Rated the DMT encounter as 'more real' than everyday waking consciousness
data_points.detail: Davis 2020; majority labelled the experience as more real than everyday reality, a hallmark 'realer than real' finding.

data_points.claim: Reported fear at some point during the DMT entity encounter
data_points.detail: Davis 2020. Fear was the most common challenging emotion but was outweighed by love/kindness/joy, the most prominent emotions both felt and attributed to the entity.

data_points.claim: Received a message, insight or directive from the DMT entity
data_points.detail: Davis 2020. A separate 19% reported receiving a prediction about the future.

data_points.claim: Believed the DMT entity continued to exist after the encounter ended
data_points.detail: Davis 2020; respondents held the entity existed in this or another dimension after the experience. (Report only; the Atlas makes no metaphysical claim.)

data_points.claim: Self-identified atheists before vs after the DMT encounter
data_points.detail: Davis 2020. More than half of respondents who identified as atheist before the experience no longer did so afterwards; the survey found significant increases in belief in ultimate reality / higher power / God / universal divinity. Reported belief shift only.

data_points.claim: Lawrence 2022 corpus of inhaled N,N-DMT experience reports analysed
data_points.detail: Ten years of r/DMT Reddit posts, quantitative content analysis; Lawrence, Carhart-Harris, Griffiths, Timmermann et al., Scientific Reports 2022;12:8562.

data_points.claim: Entity encounters reported in the Lawrence 2022 DMT corpus
data_points.detail: Lawrence 2022. Encounter rate lower than in entity-selected surveys (Davis) because this corpus was not pre-screened for entity contact.

data_points.claim: Lawrence 2022 entity phenotype: feminine
data_points.detail: Most common single phenotype among entity encounters (n=1719) in Lawrence 2022.

data_points.claim: Lawrence 2022 entity phenotype: deities / divine beings
data_points.detail: Phenotype breakdown, Lawrence 2022.

data_points.claim: Lawrence 2022 entity phenotype: aliens
data_points.detail: Phenotype breakdown, Lawrence 2022.

data_points.claim: Lawrence 2022 entity phenotype: creature-based (incl. reptilian, insectoid)
data_points.detail: Phenotype breakdown, Lawrence 2022.

data_points.claim: Lawrence 2022 entity phenotype: mythological beings (incl. 'machine elves')
data_points.detail: Phenotype breakdown, Lawrence 2022. Notably low, contradicting the popular assumption that 'machine elves' dominate DMT encounters.

data_points.claim: Lawrence 2022 entity phenotype: jesters / clowns
data_points.detail: Phenotype breakdown, Lawrence 2022.

data_points.claim: Lawrence 2022 entity interaction disposition: positive / benevolent vs guide/pedagogical vs negative
data_points.detail: Interaction-type breakdown among entity encounters (n=1719), Lawrence 2022.

data_points.claim: Michael 2021 naturalistic field-study DMT reports involving an entity/'being' encounter
data_points.detail: 'An Encounter With the Other' (Michael, Luke, Robinson, Front Psychol 2021). 36 immediate post-experience interviews; screened experienced users, 40–75 mg inhaled (mean 54.5), 83% Caucasian, mean age 37.4. Most entities (72%) were 'otherly creatures'.

data_points.claim: Michael 2021 entity demeanour: charming/inviting vs benevolent vs mischievous vs fearsome
data_points.detail: Demeanour breakdown (of 36), Michael 2021. Threatening tone was rare.

data_points.claim: Michael 2021 interaction & communication with entities
data_points.stat_or_value: helping/nurturing role 53%; showing/communing 47%; communication present 39%; explicit messages received 33%
data_points.detail: Michael 2021 (of 36). 100% (36/36) reported experiencing 'other worlds'.

data_points.claim: Michael 2025 naturalistic DMT reports containing at least one canonical NDE theme
data_points.detail: 'An encounter with death' (Michael, Luke, Robinson, Front Psychol 2025), comparing 36 DMT interviews with 34 matched NDE narratives. Concludes DMT is 'NDE-mimetic' but qualitatively distinct; 5 classical NDE features were entirely absent from DMT. (Some secondary summaries cite 95%; full-text figure is 34/36 = 94%.)

data_points.claim: Michael 2025 individual NDE-feature prevalence in DMT reports
data_points.stat_or_value: disembodiment 53% (19/36); translocation 33% (12/36); bright light(s) 25% (9/36); tunnel-like structures 28% (10/36); the void 11% (4/36)
data_points.detail: Feature-by-feature content analysis, Michael 2025. Classic NDE motifs like life-review-like (6%) and deceased-family (6%) were rare in DMT; 39% (14/36) reported ≥1 less-typical NDE motif.

data_points.claim: Strassman UNM DMT program scale (1990–1995)
data_points.detail: University of New Mexico School of Medicine, first US human psychedelic study in a generation. Dose-response work published as Strassman & Qualls, Arch Gen Psychiatry 1994 (Parts I & II). Study-history fact, not dosing guidance.

data_points.claim: Strassman's estimate of high-dose volunteers reporting contact with autonomous 'beings'
data_points.detail: Strassman (DMT: The Spirit Molecule) reported roughly half of high-dose sessions involved encounters with seemingly independent entities; qualitative estimate, not a controlled frequency count.

data_points.claim: Timmermann 2018 participants exceeding the Greyson NDE-scale threshold under DMT
data_points.detail: 'DMT Models the Near-Death Experience' (Front Psychol 2018). Within-subjects placebo-controlled, N=13; NDE cutoff is ≥7 of 32.

data_points.claim: Timmermann 2018 DMT vs placebo on total Greyson NDE score
data_points.detail: Very large effect. Ego-dissolution similarly elevated (t=6.98, d=2.67). Timmermann 2018.

data_points.claim: Timmermann 2018 overlap of DMT state with 'real' NDE reports
data_points.stat_or_value: no significant difference on 15 of 16 NDE items, all 4 subscales, and total score
data_points.detail: DMT scores compared against a matched group who had life-threatening-event NDEs (Greyson subscales: cognitive, affective, transcendental, paranormal). Timmermann 2018.

data_points.claim: Timmermann 2019 EEG spectral changes under DMT
data_points.stat_or_value: robust reduction of alpha (8–12 Hz) and beta power; increase in delta/theta at peak
data_points.detail: 'Neural correlates of the DMT experience assessed with multivariate EEG', Sci Rep 2019;9:16324, N=13, placebo-controlled. First study of pure DMT on spontaneous human brain activity.

data_points.claim: Timmermann 2019 signal-diversity / Lempel-Ziv complexity finding
data_points.stat_or_value: significant increase in LZ complexity, tracking self-reported experience intensity
data_points.detail: LZ complexity rose at the DMT peak and correlated with 'immersiveness' and visual-imagery ratings; consistent with the entropic-brain framework. Timmermann 2019.

data_points.claim: Timmermann 2023 EEG-fMRI study of DMT: sample and dose
data_points.stat_or_value: N = 20; 20 mg IV bolus vs placebo
data_points.detail: 'Human brain effects of DMT assessed via EEG-fMRI', PNAS 2023;120(13):e2218949120. Simultaneous EEG-fMRI before/during/after. Dose is a study-administration fact.

data_points.claim: Timmermann 2023 whole-brain fMRI signature of DMT
data_points.stat_or_value: robust increase in global functional connectivity (GFC); network disintegration/desegregation; compression of the principal cortical gradient
data_points.detail: GFC × subjective-intensity maps spatially correlated with independent PET-derived 5-HT2A receptor density maps. Timmermann 2023.

data_points.claim: Alamia 2020: DMT reverses the direction of cortical travelling waves
data_points.stat_or_value: backward waves decreased (~0.47 → ~0.18 dB); forward waves increased (~0 → ~0.19 dB)
data_points.detail: 'DMT alters cortical travelling waves', eLife 2020;9:e59784, N=13 (dose groups 7–20 mg IV). Effects peaked 2–5 min post-injection and faded by ~10 min.

data_points.claim: Alamia 2020 alpha-band and slow-band Bayesian evidence under DMT
data_points.stat_or_value: alpha reduction BF10 = 16.04; delta increase BF10 = 391.16; theta increase BF10 = 19.23
data_points.detail: Bayesian factors for spectral change, Alamia 2020. Alpha suppression is among the most reliable neurophysiological signatures of the psychedelic state.

data_points.claim: Lyke 2019 Erowid DMT entity-report analysis: base rates
data_points.stat_or_value: 149 reports (2006–2015); 75% described ≥1 entity; 180 total entities coded
data_points.detail: Lyke (2019), Erowid trip-report content analysis; sample 90% male, mean age 24.6. 37% of reports described multiple entities. Most common interaction type was showing/teaching/guidance (25%).

data_points.claim: Lyke 2019 entity-type frequencies
data_points.stat_or_value: poorly-defined/formless 29%; humanoid 22%; divine 10%; aliens 8%; elves/fairies 7%; animals 6%; geometric/machine 6%
data_points.detail: Lyke 2019. 'Machine elves' were among the least common phenotypes—most entities were amorphous or generic ('not everyone gets machine elves'). Gender specified in only 24% of descriptions, more often female.

data_points.claim: Sanz 2018 NLP corpus: hallucinogens rank highest in semantic similarity to dreaming
data_points.stat_or_value: 165 substances compared; ANOVA of drug category on dream-similarity F = 31.34, p < 0.0001
data_points.detail: 'The Experience Elicited by Hallucinogens Presents the Highest Similarity to Dreaming...', Front Neurosci 2018;12:7. Semantic analysis of Erowid reports vs ~200,000 dream-journal reports.

data_points.claim: Sanz 2018: where DMT ranked for dream-similarity
data_points.detail: Sanz 2018. DMT high on similarity but exceeded by LSD and some deliriants (e.g., Datura ranked #1 for low-lucidity dreams).

data_points.claim: Martial 2019 NLP: which drug's reports most resemble near-death experiences
data_points.stat_or_value: ketamine ranked #1 of 165 substances; DMT ranked lower
data_points.detail: 'Neurochemical models of near-death experiences', Conscious Cogn 2019;69:52–69. ~15,000 Erowid reports vs 625 NDE narratives; ketamine most similar, then Salvia, then serotonergic psychedelics incl. DMT. Tension with Timmermann 2018 (which frames DMT as an NDE model) — both included for the disagreement.

data_points.claim: Cakic 2010 Australian DMT user survey: route of use
data_points.detail: Cakic, Potkonyak & Marshall, Drug Alcohol Depend 2010;111(1):30–37. Online survey of recreational users; increased psychospiritual insight reported by 75.5% (smoked).

data_points.claim: Winstock 2014 Global Drug Survey DMT prevalence
data_points.detail: Winstock, Kaar & Borschmann, J Psychopharmacol 2014;28(1):49–54. Large global self-selected sample (~22,000).

data_points.claim: Winstock 2014: DMT abuse-liability / urge-to-use-more
data_points.stat_or_value: 'urge to use more' = 1.3 (lowest of drugs compared)
data_points.detail: Winstock 2014. Despite a very desirable effect profile, DMT showed the lowest urge to redose among compared drugs (e.g., ketamine ~3.0). Reported epidemiology only; no use guidance.

data_points.claim: Barsuglia 2018 5-MeO-DMT retreat study: complete mystical experience rate
data_points.stat_or_value: 75% (15 of 20) had a 'complete' mystical experience; MEQ30 overall M = 4.17 (SD 0.64, 0–5 scale)
data_points.detail: DISTINCT COMPOUND — 5-MeO-DMT (toad-derived), NOT N,N-DMT. Barsuglia et al., Front Psychol 2018;9:2459; n=20; intensity statistically comparable to a high-dose (30 mg/70 kg) lab psilocybin session. Included for cross-compound contrast.

data_points.claim: Cott & Rock 2008 early DMT phenomenology survey
data_points.stat_or_value: 19 reports coded → 9 thematic categories; sample mean age 23, 95% male
data_points.detail: Cott & Rock, J Sci Explor 2008. Themes: hallucinations, lucidity, affective distortions, ineffability, extreme intensity, spirituality, space-time/self distortion, familiarity, entering other realities inhabited by sentient beings.

data_points.claim: Luan 2024 first extended-state (continuous-infusion) DMT study: design
data_points.stat_or_value: 11 volunteers; up to 4 dose levels; ~30-min bolus-plus-infusion; single-blind, placebo-controlled, within-subjects
data_points.detail: 'Psychological and physiological effects of extended DMT', Luan, Eckernäs, ... Carhart-Harris, Timmermann, J Psychopharmacol 2024. Operationalises the Gallimore & Strassman (2016, Front Pharmacol 7:211) target-controlled-infusion model. Doses are study-administration facts.

data_points.claim: Luan 2024 extended-DMT phenomenology and safety findings
data_points.stat_or_value: entity encounters increased in the latter part of infusion; ego dissolution stayed minimal; short-term psychological tolerance; heart rate & anxiety stabilised to normal
data_points.detail: Luan 2024. Subjective intensity plateaued while plasma DMT rose slightly (apparent habituation). No serious adverse events; supports feasibility of a sustained DMT state.

data_points.claim: QRI 'hyperbolic geometry of DMT' model (theory, not empirical measurement)
data_points.stat_or_value: 6 dose-ordered phases; claims all 17 wallpaper symmetry groups (and up to 230 crystallographic space groups) can manifest
data_points.detail: Qualia Research Institute (Gomez-Emilsson). Phases: threshold → chrysanthemum → 'magic eye' → waiting room → breakthrough → amnesia; posits phenomenal space acquires negative (hyperbolic) curvature that intensifies with intensity. LABELLED AS SPECULATIVE THEORY — no metaphysical or measured-curvature claim endorsed by the Atlas.

sources_new.author: Timmermann C, Roseman L, Haijen E, Carhart-Harris RL, et al.
sources_new.work: Human brain effects of DMT assessed via EEG-fMRI (PNAS 120(13):e2218949120)
sources_new.note: N=20, 20 mg IV DMT; simultaneous EEG-fMRI; GFC increase, cortical-gradient compression, 5-HT2AR spatial correlation.

sources_new.author: Alamia A, Timmermann C, Nutt DJ, VanRullen R, Carhart-Harris RL
sources_new.note: N=13; DMT decreases backward and increases forward travelling waves; alpha suppression (BF10=16.04).

sources_new.author: Sanz C, Zamberlan F, Erowid E, Erowid F, Tagliazucchi E
sources_new.work: The Experience Elicited by Hallucinogens Presents the Highest Similarity to Dreaming within a Large Database of Psychoactive Substance Reports (Front Neurosci 12:7)
sources_new.note: 165 substances vs dream-journal corpus; ANOVA F=31.34, p<0.0001; DMT high but below LSD/deliriants.

sources_new.author: Martial C, Cassol H, Charland-Verville V, ... Tagliazucchi E, Laureys S
sources_new.work: Neurochemical models of near-death experiences: a large-scale study based on the semantic similarity of written reports (Conscious Cogn 69:52-69)
sources_new.note: ~15,000 Erowid reports across 165 substances vs 625 NDE narratives; ketamine most NDE-like, DMT lower. Tension with Timmermann 2018.

sources_new.author: Cakic V, Potkonyak J, Marshall A
sources_new.work: Dimethyltryptamine (DMT): subjective effects and patterns of use among Australian recreational users (Drug Alcohol Depend 111(1):30-37)
sources_new.note: n=121; smoking 98.3%, ayahuasca 30.6%; psychospiritual insight 75.5%; anxiety/stress 10.9%.

sources_new.author: Winstock AR, Kaar S, Borschmann R
sources_new.work: Dimethyltryptamine (DMT): prevalence, user characteristics and abuse liability in a large global sample (J Psychopharmacol 28(1):49-54)
sources_new.note: Lifetime 8.9% (n=1980), past-year 5.0% (n=1123); lowest urge-to-redose (1.3) of compared drugs.

sources_new.author: Barsuglia J, Davis AK, Palmer R, ... Griffiths RR
sources_new.work: Intensity of Mystical Experiences Occasioned by 5-MeO-DMT and Comparison With a Prior Psilocybin Study (Front Psychol 9:2459)
sources_new.note: DISTINCT COMPOUND 5-MeO-DMT (not N,N-DMT). n=20; 75% complete mystical experience; MEQ30 M=4.17.

sources_new.author: Cott C, Rock A
sources_new.work: Phenomenology of N,N-Dimethyltryptamine Use: A Thematic Analysis (J Sci Explor 22(3))
sources_new.note: 19 online reports; 9 themes; mean age 23, 95% male. Early systematic DMT phenomenology.

sources_new.author: Luan LX, Eckernäs E, Ashton M, Rosas FE, ... Carhart-Harris RL, Timmermann C
sources_new.work: Psychological and physiological effects of extended DMT (J Psychopharmacol)
sources_new.note: First extended-state DMT study; 11 volunteers, up to 4 dose levels, ~30-min bolus+infusion; entity encounters rose late, minimal ego dissolution, short-term psychological tolerance.
